sources.notes: Counts binary strings such that every constrained interval is beautiful; n and m sums are up to 3e5.
Official tutorial code converts constraints into max_left bounds and uses prefix-sum optimized DP.

annotation.summary: Count binary strings of length n for which every given interval can have exactly one block removed so the remaining interval has an odd number of blocks.
annotation.constraints: Sum of n and m is 3e5, requiring a linear or near-linear DP over positions.
annotation.core_idea: The beauty condition for an interval translates into a lower bound on the previous split/transition position. For each right endpoint, keep the maximum forbidden-left boundary, then count valid strings ending at each position with a DP whose transition range is a suffix of previous states, answered by prefix sums.
tricks: reduce interval constraints to prefix maximum boundary

solution_variants.name: prefix-bound-dp
solution_variants.summary: Store max_left[r], propagate prefix maxima, and compute dp[i] from a valid range of earlier split positions using prefix sums.

tags.evidence: The recurrence sums a contiguous range of previous dp states and is made linear with prefix sums.
tags.description: Dynamic programming where range-sum transitions are optimized using prefix sums or prefix aggregates.
tags.created_reason: The existing DP tag is too coarse for range-sum transition optimization.

tags.evidence: The DP counts binary strings satisfying all interval constraints modulo 998244353.
All interval constraints are reduced to a running maximum left boundary before the DP is performed.
tags.description: Transform local/range constraints into prefix-style accumulated bounds or prefix aggregates.
tags.created_reason: This reusable reduction is more specific than a generic implementation or data-structure tag.
